usage_sections: Quick start: > :informationsource: We recommend using pipx over pip for system-wide installations. In order to grab the latest stable release run: python3 -m pipx install impacket If you want to play with the unreleased changes, download the development version from the master branch, extract

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: --access
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: -aesKey
arguments.description: AES key to use for Kerberos Authentication (128 or 256 bits)

arguments.name: -base-dir
arguments.description: Directory to search in (Default: /)

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: --create
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: -dc-ip
arguments.description: IP Address of the domain controller. If omitted it will use the domain part (FQDN) specified in the target parameter

arguments.name: -debug
arguments.description: Turn DEBUG output ON

arguments.name: -domain
arguments.description: <domain name>

arguments.name: -hashes
arguments.description: NTLM hashes, format is LMHASH:NTHASH

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication. Grabs credentials from ccache file (KRB5CCNAME) based on target parameters. If valid credentials cannot be found, it will use the ones specified in the command line

arguments.name: -m
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: --modify
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: -no-pass
arguments.description: Don

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Destination port to connect to the SMB Server.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Destination port to connect to the SMB Server.

arguments.name: -port
arguments.description: Destination port to connect to SMB Server

arguments.name: -rpc-hostname
arguments.description: # Our RPC over HTTP v2 implementation tries to extract the

arguments.name: -share
arguments.description: SMB Share

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: Set connection timeout (seconds).

arguments.name: -target-ip
arguments.description: IP Address of the target machine. If omitted it will use whatever was specified as target. This is useful when target is the NetBIOS name and you cannot resolve it

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Set connection timeout (seconds).

arguments.name: -timeout
arguments.description: DNS timeout

arguments.name: --timestamp
arguments.description: Specify a timestamp to set for the selected filetimes (format: YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS.mmmmmm).

arguments.name: -ts
arguments.description: Adds timestamp to every logging output

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Query the file after touching to verify the changes.

arguments.name: --validate
arguments.description: Query the file after touching to verify the changes.

arguments.name: -w
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: --write
arguments.description: Change the

arguments.name: -xmlfile
arguments.description: Group Policy Preferences XML files to parse

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

arguments.name: --filter
arguments.description: BPF filter expression for packet selection.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with fortra/nanodump (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with fortra/CVE-2024-0204 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
